diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Passive continuous leakage from an over-distended bladder is the hallmark of overflow incontinence.
OAB produces urge-driven larger-volume voids, not passive constant dribble from a full bladder.

symptoms.symptom_name: Near-anuria — only drops passed despite frequent attempts and constant urge; sensation of incomplete emptying

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to void a meaningful volume with a persistent full sensation is the defining feature of retention.
UTI can cause frequency but not absolute inability to void a meaningful volume.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severe escalating suprapubic pain with swollen/hard lower abdomen

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid painful over-distension marks acute (rather than chronic painless) retention and drives the urgency.
Anticholinergic detrusor blockade is a well-recognised pharmacological precipitant of acute retention in older adults.

symptoms.symptom_name: Palpable tense suprapubic mass extending to the umbilicus, dull to percussion (distended bladder)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A palpable, percussion-dull midline suprapubic mass is a distended bladder — sealed clinician exam finding; the AI Doctor cannot palpate or percuss.

symptoms.symptom_name: 1200 mL clear urine drained on urethral catheterisation

investigation_details.expected_result: Large residual (~1200 mL) confirming acute retention; immediate relief of pain and overflow
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Retention undrained — ongoing pain, obstructive AKI, rupture risk

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A large drained volume confirms retention — but this is the clinician's decompressive procedure, never a telehealth AI action.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated post-void residual on bladder scan / urodynamics

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Quantifies retention; requires equipment the AI Doctor does not have.

investigation_details.expected_result: May show post-renal (obstructive) acute kidney injury

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Bloods detect obstructive AKI from retrograde pressure — unavailable in telehealth until an in-person/lab pathway is opened.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, no rigors, no gross haematuria

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fever/rigors makes concurrent urosepsis less likely now, but does not exclude later infection.

symptoms.symptom_name: No saddle anaesthesia, no lower-limb weakness, normal bowels

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Intact perineal sensation and leg power make a neurogenic (cord) cause of retention unlikely — but it must always be screened.

symptoms.symptom_name: Long-standing prior mixed stress and urge incontinence

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The chronic incontinence baseline is the red herring — it frames the presentation as benign but is not the acute problem.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute urinary retention with overflow incontinence
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Continuous passive dribble + near-anuria despite urge + severe suprapubic pain together identify overflow from an over-distended bladder, distinguishing it from benign worsening incontinence.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Urethral catheterisation for bladder decompression (in person)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Both diagnostic and therapeutic — drains the bladder and relieves the crisis; must be performed in person, not by the AI Doctor.
Confirms and quantifies retention where catheterisation is not immediately performed.
Detects post-renal (obstructive) acute kidney injury after retention.
Screens for infection, especially once an indwelling catheter is placed.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Empirical broad antibiotics without evidence of infection
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No fever/rigors/dysuria — antibiotics are not indicated for uncomplicated retention and should not be given reflexively.
